proof_sketch: Under fixed shape M=16384, K=16, N=16384, original grid_x = (N+63)/64 = 256, mutated grid_x = (N+63)*64 = 1,052,608. The kernel writes output only when block_col = blockIdx.x * TILE_N < N. For blockIdx.x >= 256, block_col >= 16384, so no writes occur. All valid output tiles (blockIdx.x = 0..255) are computed identically; extra blocks produce zeros and do not modify output. Thus output is bitwise identical for all input values.

But load_row = block_row + thread_row + i, and block_row is from blockIdx.y*TILE_M, so block_row is within M (since blockIdx.y max 255, TILE_M=64, block_row max 255*64=16320, plus thread_row up to 60, so max 16380, still less than M=16384).
